title: The Spectacular
author: Fiona Davis
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Spectacular" book by Fiona Davis?
Options:
A. I'm 19 years old and unmarried. And I definitely won't be getting married soon, based on my last talk with Nathaniel. Great. The room and board fee includes two home-cooked meals daily. On weekends, you'll need to fend for yourself. Anyone residing here must be working towards a career in theater, like taking lessons, seeking an agent, or trying out for roles. You already have employment, so that requirement is met. The weekly rate is $18. Let me give you a tour. She showed them the practice area, where one woman was playing a beat-up upright piano while another sang loudly in a robust alto voice.  
B. I'm 19 and single. And I'm definitely not getting married soon after that last talk with Nathaniel. Great. Room and board here includes two home-cooked meals daily. You'll have to fend for yourself food-wise on weekends. To live here, you have to be working toward a career in theater - taking classes, seeking an agent, auditioning, etc. You already have a job, so you're all set there. It's $18 a week. Let me give you a tour. She showed them the practice area where one woman pounded on a beat-up old piano while another sang loudly in a bold alto voice.
C. Im nineteen and not married. And certainly not getting married anytime soon, after her last conversation with Nathaniel. Excellent. Room and board includes two home-cooked meals a day. On weekends, youre on your own. Anyone living here has to be pursuing a career on the stage, whether taking classes, looking for an agent, or auditioning. You already have a job, so thats covered. The cost is eighteen dollars a week. Let me show you around. The tour took them to the practice room, where one girl was banging away on a scuffed upright piano while another belted out a song in a strong alto.
D. At 19, I'm not married, and I certainly don't plan on it anytime soon after that last discussion with Nathaniel. Wonderful. The room and board rate covers two homemade meals per day. You'll be on your own for food on weekends. To live here, you must be pursuing some kind of theatrical career by taking classes, looking for an agent, or auditioning. Since you already work, you meet that prerequisite. It's $18 per week. Let me show you around. The tour included the practice room, where one girl banged on an old, scuffed piano as another belted out a song in a strong alto voice.
